message:
beads: file fcp-ratelimit review findings (tk9j5, e8v7i, 83xt1)

Closing the fcp-ratelimit bucket persistence + bypass review with
three follow-up beads, in descending severity:

  tk9j5 [CRITICAL] bucket state is in-memory only; process restart
                   resets every pool to full capacity -- trivial
                   bypass for any client that can trigger a crash
                   or wait for natural churn. manifest `scope`
                   declarations (instance/credential/global) are
                   all local-process right now.

  e8v7i [HIGH]     PoolState in fcp-sdk uses a hard fixed-window
                   reset -- classic boundary doubling attack
                   (2 * limit within 2 * epsilon at window edge).
                   TokenBucket's continuous refill model is fine;
                   the exposed-to-manifest tracker is the one that
                   doubles.

  83xt1 [MEDIUM]   RateLimitTracker::try_consume silently skips
                   unregistered pool ids instead of failing closed.
                   Current call paths are safe (manifest validator
                   rejects unknown pools) but from_declarations
                   accepts unvalidated decls -- wrong default for
                   an admission-control primitive.

Also noted two LOW correctness items (enforce() state-read race in
fcp-ratelimit/src/fcp.rs:259-260, TokenBucket refill stale-read at
token_bucket.rs:127-134). Not worth filing; code-comment-grade.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
